agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer money transfers with regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), tiered account verification, and fraud prevention controls. Operates in a regulated financial services environment where identity verification, sanctions screening, and transaction finality are legally mandated.","bounded_context":"Digital Wallet — Transfers & Account Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"KYC (Know Your Customer)","definition":"Identity verification process required before a user can send money or withdraw funds. Determines account verification tier."},{"term":"Verified Account","definition":"An account that has completed full KYC identity verification. Eligible to send, receive, and withdraw funds up to the full daily limit."},{"term":"Partially-Verified Account","definition":"An account with incomplete KYC — has provided some identity information but not completed full verification. Can send up to a reduced daily limit but cannot withdraw."},{"term":"Unverified Account","definition":"An account with no KYC completion. Can receive money only — cannot send or withdraw."},{"term":"Settlement","definition":"The finalization of a transaction, occurring at T+1 (one business day after initiation). Once settled, a transaction is irreversible."},{"term":"T+1","definition":"Settlement window — one business day after the transaction is initiated."},{"term":"Sanctioned Entity","definition":"An individual or organization appearing on a regulatory sanctions list (e.g., OFAC, EU sanctions). All transfers to or from sanctioned entities must be blocked."},{"term":"Account Lock","definition":"A security state triggered by 5 consecutive failed login attempts. Only a support agent can unlock the account; no self-service unlock path exists."},{"term":"Daily Transfer Limit","definition":"The maximum cumulative amount a user can send in a calendar day, determined by their verification tier."},{"term":"Transaction Reversal","definition":"The act of undoing a completed transaction. Only possible before settlement (pre-T+1)."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Users must complete KYC identity verification before they can send money.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Regulatory requirement — sending money without identity verification violates AML/KYC laws. System integrity is compromised if an unverified user can initiate an outbound transfer.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create an outbound transfer from an unverified account MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Unverified accounts can receive money but cannot send or withdraw.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Regulatory constraint on fund movement. Allowing unverified accounts to move money out violates compliance requirements. Receiving is permitted to avoid blocking legitimate inbound payments.","testable_assertion":"An unverified account can have its balance credited via an inbound transfer. Any attempt to send or withdraw from an unverified account MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Daily transfer limit for fully verified accounts is $5,000.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business policy threshold. A VP or compliance officer could adjust this limit based on risk appetite or market conditions without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers from a verified account in a calendar day MUST NOT exceed $5,000. Any transfer that would exceed this cumulative limit MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Daily transfer limit for partially-verified accounts is $500.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Business policy threshold, adjustable by policy decision. The specific dollar amount is a configurable risk parameter.","testable_assertion":"The sum of all outbound transfers from a partially-verified account in a calendar day MUST NOT exceed $500."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A transaction cannot be reversed once it has been settled (T+1).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Transaction finality is a core financial invariant. Reversing a settled transaction would corrupt ledger integrity, create double-spend scenarios, and violate settlement guarantees to counterparties.","testable_assertion":"Any reversal request for a transaction whose settlement timestamp is in the past MUST be rejected. The system MUST NOT alter the ledger entry of a settled transaction."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked automatically before execution.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Legal/regulatory mandate. Allowing a transfer to a sanctioned entity is a criminal violation (e.g., OFAC). This is not a policy preference — it is a legal constraint that, if violated, exposes the company to criminal liability.","testable_assertion":"Before any transfer is executed, the recipient MUST be screened against the active sanctions list. If the recipient matches, the transfer MUST be blocked and the event MUST be logged for compliance audit."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"5 consecutive failed login attempts lock the account. Only a support agent can unlock it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Security integrity constraint. If the system allows login after 5 consecutive failures or permits self-service unlock, the fraud prevention mechanism is defeated. The support-agent-only unlock is a deliberate friction point for security.","testable_assertion":"After 5 consecutive failed login attempts, the account status MUST transition to 'locked'. Subsequent login attempts with correct credentials MUST still be rejected while locked. Only an authenticated support agent action can transition the account back to 'unlocked'."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"A notification must be sent for any transaction over $1,000.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a UX/communication policy. The threshold could be changed, notification channels could vary, and failure to notify does not corrupt system state or violate law. It is a business preference for transparency.","testable_assertion":"When a transaction with amount > $1,000 is completed, a notification event MUST be emitted. Failure to send does not block the transaction."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A user with an unverified account","when":"they attempt to send money to another user","then":"the transfer is rejected with a clear error indicating KYC verification is required"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A user with an unverified account","when":"another user sends them money","then":"the funds are credited to the unverified account's balance successfully"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A user with an unverified account","when":"they attempt to withdraw funds","then":"the withdrawal is rejected with a clear error indicating KYC verification is required"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A verified user who has already sent $4,800 today","when":"they attempt to send $300","then":"the transfer is rejected because it would exceed the $5,000 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A verified user who has already sent $4,800 today","when":"they attempt to send $200","then":"the transfer succeeds as the cumulative total ($5,000) does not exceed the limit"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A partially-verified user who has already sent $400 today","when":"they attempt to send $150","then":"the transfer is rejected because it would exceed the $500 daily limit"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A transaction that was initiated yesterday and has settled (T+1 has passed)","when":"the sender requests a reversal","then":"the reversal is rejected with a message indicating the transaction is settled and irreversible"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A transaction that was initiated today and has not yet settled","when":"the sender requests a reversal","then":"the reversal is permitted (pre-settlement window)"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A user attempts to transfer funds to a recipient","when":"the recipient matches an entry on the sanctions list","then":"the transfer is blocked, the user is informed, and the event is logged for compliance audit"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A user has failed login 4 times consecutively","when":"they fail a 5th time","then":"the account is locked and the user is informed that support must unlock it"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A user account is locked due to failed login attempts","when":"the user enters the correct password","then":"login is still rejected because the account is locked"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A user account is locked","when":"a support agent issues an unlock command","then":"the account is unlocked and the user can log in with correct credentials"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A verified user sends a transfer of $1,500","when":"the transaction completes successfully","then":"a notification is sent to the user about the transaction"},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A verified user sends a transfer of $999","when":"the transaction completes successfully","then":"no large-transaction notification is triggered"},{"id":"AC-15","given":"A partially-verified user","when":"they attempt to send money","then":"the transfer is allowed up to their $500 daily limit (partial KYC grants limited send capability)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A user completes KYC verification while they have a pending inbound transfer","expected_behavior":"The inbound transfer completes normally. The newly verified status applies only to future outbound actions — it does not retroactively alter pending inbound transactions."},{"scenario":"A transfer is initiated to a recipient who becomes sanctioned between initiation and settlement","expected_behavior":"The sanctions check should be performed at execution time. If the recipient is sanctioned at the point of settlement/execution, the transfer must be blocked. This implies a re-screening step before settlement."},{"scenario":"A user sends exactly $5,000 in a single transaction on the boundary of a calendar day (11:59 PM)","expected_behavior":"The daily limit resets at the start of the next calendar day. The timezone used for 'calendar day' must be explicitly defined (likely UTC or the user's registered timezone)."},{"scenario":"A locked user's 6th, 7th, etc. login attempts","expected_behavior":"These are still rejected. The failed attempt counter does not reset or increment further — the account remains locked regardless of additional attempts until a support agent unlocks it."},{"scenario":"A user receives $10,000 while unverified","expected_behavior":"The inbound transfer succeeds and the balance reflects $10,000. However, the user cannot send or withdraw any of it until they complete KYC."},{"scenario":"Transaction amount is exactly $1,000","expected_behavior":"The rule states 'over $1,000' — a $1,000.00 transaction should NOT trigger the notification. Only amounts strictly greater than $1,000.00 trigger it."},{"scenario":"A support agent attempts to unlock an account that is not locked","expected_behavior":"The operation should be idempotent — no error, account remains unlocked."},{"scenario":"A partially-verified user attempts to withdraw","expected_behavior":"The spec says unverified accounts cannot withdraw but does not explicitly state whether partially-verified accounts can. This is ambiguous and requires clarification."},{"scenario":"Multiple concurrent transfers that individually are under the daily limit but collectively exceed it","expected_behavior":"The system must enforce the daily limit atomically. If two $3,000 transfers from a verified account are submitted simultaneously, at most one should succeed. Race conditions must not allow the limit to be bypassed."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Direct revenue impact from transfer fees. Incorrect limit enforcement could lead to regulatory fines (sanctions violations carry penalties in the millions). Overly restrictive limits reduce transaction volume and fee revenue.","operational":"KYC verification creates onboarding friction — conversion rate from unverified to verified is a key operational metric. Account locking generates support ticket volume; the no-self-service-unlock policy is a deliberate security-over-convenience tradeoff that increases support costs.","user":"Unverified users may be frustrated by restrictions on sending/withdrawing. Locked accounts with no self-service path create friction. Notification for >$1,000 transactions builds trust and transparency.","risk":"Sanctions screening failure is the highest-risk item — criminal liability and license revocation. Settlement finality violations would undermine ledger integrity. Daily limit bypass via race conditions is a fraud vector.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"End Users (Wallet Holders)","impact":"Subject to verification tiers, transfer limits, and account locking. Must complete KYC to access full functionality."},{"group":"Compliance / Legal Team","impact":"Owns sanctions list updates, KYC policy, and audit requirements. Must receive logs of all blocked sanctions transfers."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Sole authority to unlock accounts. Will receive increased ticket volume from locked accounts and KYC-related inquiries."},{"group":"Finance / Treasury","impact":"Settlement finality (T+1) affects cash flow management and reconciliation. Daily limits affect liquidity planning."},{"group":"Fraud / Risk Team","impact":"Account locking and daily limits are primary fraud prevention controls. Must monitor for limit bypass attempts and suspicious patterns."},{"group":"Regulators","impact":"KYC, sanctions screening, and transaction monitoring are regulatory requirements. Audit trail completeness is critical for examinations."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is reasonably clear on most rules but has notable gaps: (1) partially-verified accounts' withdrawal rights are not explicitly stated, (2) the timezone for 'daily' limit reset is undefined, (3) whether sanctions screening happens only at initiation or also at settlement is ambiguous, (4) T+1 is stated but whether this means business days or calendar days is unspecified, (5) the notification rule for >$1,000 does not specify channel or failure handling. No outright contradictions exist, but several ambiguities prevent full confidence.","high_confidence_areas":["KYC required before sending — clear and unambiguous","Unverified accounts can receive but not send/withdraw","5 failed logins lock the account, support-only unlock","Settled transactions cannot be reversed","Sanctions screening must block transfers"],"low_confidence_areas":["Partially-verified account withdrawal rights (not stated)","Timezone definition for daily limit reset","Whether sanctions re-screening occurs at settlement","T+1 meaning: business days vs calendar days","Notification channel and failure handling for >$1,000 transactions","Whether $1,000 exactly triggers notification (boundary ambiguity)"],"assumptions":["Partially-verified accounts can send (up to $500/day) but withdrawal rights are unclear — assumed restricted like unverified","Daily limit is cumulative across all outbound transfers in a calendar day","T+1 refers to one business day","Sanctions screening occurs at least at transaction initiation","Notification for >$1,000 is strictly greater than, not greater-than-or-equal"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec defines restrictions for 'unverified' and limits for 'partially-verified' accounts but does not explicitly state whether partially-verified accounts can withdraw. Unverified accounts explicitly cannot withdraw — does partial verification grant withdrawal rights or only send rights?","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Compliance","Customer Support"],"options":["A: Partially-verified accounts can withdraw up to their $500 daily limit","B: Partially-verified accounts cannot withdraw — only fully verified accounts can withdraw","C: Partially-verified accounts can withdraw with a separate, lower limit"],"recommendation":"Option B — require full verification for withdrawals. Withdrawals convert digital balance to real-world funds and carry higher fraud risk than peer-to-peer transfers. Full KYC should be required."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The 'daily transfer limit' does not specify which timezone defines a 'day' or when the limit resets. For a global user base, this is critical for consistent enforcement.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Engineering","Fraud/Risk Team"],"options":["A: UTC-based calendar day","B: User's registered timezone","C: Rolling 24-hour window from first transfer"],"recommendation":"Option A — UTC-based calendar day. Simplest to implement consistently, avoids timezone manipulation, and is standard in financial systems."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"T+1 settlement is stated but it is unclear whether this means business days (excluding weekends/holidays) or calendar days. This affects when transactions become irreversible.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Finance/Treasury","Legal"],"options":["A: T+1 business days (settlement delayed over weekends)","B: T+1 calendar days (settlement occurs 24 hours after initiation regardless)"],"recommendation":"Option B for a digital wallet context — calendar days. Traditional banking uses business days, but digital wallets typically operate 24/7 and users expect consistent behavior."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer money transfers with regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), tiered account verification, and fraud prevention controls. Operates in a regulated financial services environment where identity verification, sanctions screening, and transaction finality are legally mandated.","bounded_context":"Digital Wallet — Transfers & Account Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"KYC (Know Your Customer)","definition":"Identit
